summary: P1 backend validation of live driver location streaming flow — all 14 pytest cases pass (100%). Validated driver login, GPS POST (auth/validation/upsert + booking mirror), customer rider-poll, admin live-drivers list, lazy geocoding of pickup/dropoff, _id exclusion across every endpoint, and full trip_status lifecycle propagation (assigned → en_route → on_location → passenger_onboard → completed).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 6592 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into modules (admin, customer, driver, payments, geocoding) for maintainability. Pre-existing tech debt, not in-scope of this task.

POST /api/driver-auth/location does not validate that active_booking_id belongs to the authenticated driver before mirroring (the update_one filters on driver_id, so it is a silent no-op if the driver tries to spoof someone else's booking — safe, but worth returning 403 explicitly for clearer mobile debugging).

GET /api/customer/bookings/{id}/driver-location lazy-geocodes pickup/dropoff on every poll-before-cache, which can spike Google Maps cost if a customer polls every 5s while geocoder is briefly unreachable (returns None and doesn't cache). Consider a negative-cache TTL.
/api/admin/drivers/live returns up to 200 rows but has no pagination contract — fine today, but driver fleet >200 will silently truncate.
test_credentials: Driver: driver.test@turanelitelimo.com / DriverPass123! (worked on first try). Customer: fresh signup driverloctest_{ts}@test.com / TestPass123! (note: /api/customer/signup expects 'name' field, NOT first_name/last_name; response uses 'user' key, NOT 'customer'). Admin: JWT minted directly via JWT_SECRET to bypass 2FA email.
seed_data_creation: Each test run inserts ONE booking into MongoDB linking the seeded driver_id ↔ a freshly signed-up customer_id, with trip_status='assigned'. Booking + driver_locations entry are deleted on fixture teardown. Booking IDs are random uuid4 so re-runs are isolated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Driver-location flow is fully working end-to-end. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_driver_location_flow.py is reusable — uses Motor for direct DB seed/cleanup and mints an admin JWT to bypass 2FA. Key gotchas discovered: (1) /api/customer/signup requires 'name' field (single string), not first_name/last_name; (2) response key is 'user', not 'customer'; (3) /api/admin/bookings/{id}/assign-driver only sets driver_id if the payload phone matches an existing driver in db.drivers, so for tests where you need the driver↔booking link, direct DB seed is faster than going through the assign-driver endpoint.
rca_of_the_issue: No issue to RCA — feature works as specified. The booking mirror (driver_latitude/longitude/heading on bookings doc) correctly updates ONLY when active_booking_id is provided AND the booking's driver_id matches the authenticated driver. Customer-facing endpoint correctly returns 404 when booking is not owned. Admin /drivers/live correctly computes stale_seconds and is_online flag (<120s). _id is excluded from every response.
